verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is a secreted protein of the cysteine-rich secretory protein family, expressed in the epididymis and secreted into the epididymal lumen. The NCBI summary explicitly identifies it as a secreted protein involved in sperm-egg recognition and binding through soluble interactions. No membrane-anchoring mechanism (TM domain, GPI anchor, or outer-leaflet lipidation) is documented. The protein mediates cell-cell interactions through reversible non-covalent binding to complementary molecules, consistent with a secreted ligand in equilibrium with the soluble pool rather than a stable surface-anchored form. Germline/reproductive-cell expression does not override the secreted-only classification when the protein lacks a documented stable membrane anchor.
